summary: Narrow proposal to add a final-award-result preempt/guard in src/generator.py for unsupported final evaluation score, selected vendor, and final award-result questions while preserving legitimate evaluation-criteria explanations and normal scope answers with final-result caveats.

outputs.description: Required worker proposal contract for the unsupported award-result guard boundary repair.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Add a tighter award-result-specific preempt branch before the current generic sensitive preempt. Trigger only when the query combines unsupported final-result intent with procurement outcome intent, such as final evaluation score, selected vendor, final award result, or winner selection, and explicitly exclude criteria/rubric/breakdown explanations from this preempt path.
details: Introduce a dedicated marker set for unsupported final-award-result questions instead of widening the existing generic sensitive marker list.
Gate the preempt on a result-intent + unsupported-result combination so normal scope answers that merely mention an award caveat still flow through the model.
Add explicit negative markers for criteria, scoring rubric, evaluation method, and selection basis language so legitimate evaluation-criteria explanations remain answerable.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Keep the post-generation guard as a compression-only fallback for already-refusal answers, but do not let it reclassify or suppress criteria explanations. If the answer is about evaluation criteria or method, leave it untouched even when the query mentions final-result vocabulary.
details: Preserve the current 'only shorten verbose refusals' behavior for unsupported award-result outputs.
Avoid adding any rewrite path that strips substantive evaluation-criteria content from normal scope answers.
Keep the guard downstream of generation so it can only clean up refusal length, not change answer intent.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Invoke the new award-result preempt before the OpenAI completion call, alongside the existing sensitive preempt, so unsupported final-result questions return the concise unavailable answer without spending a model call.
details: This is the lowest-risk placement because it avoids changing retrieval, prompt construction, or answer shaping for unrelated questions.
It should not affect normal scope answers because only the unsupported final-result branch short-circuits the model call.
Leave the existing answer-guard stack intact for non-preempted queries.

expected_latency_quality_effects.scope: Unsupported final evaluation score / selected vendor / final award-result questions
expected_latency_quality_effects.latency_effect: Expected material latency reduction because the query returns before the completion call; the win is roughly one full generation round-trip for these cases.
expected_latency_quality_effects.quality_effect: Expected quality improvement from eliminating long, off-scope, or over-refusing final-result answers while still returning the concise unavailable response.

expected_latency_quality_effects.latency_effect: No meaningful regression expected if the exclusion markers are implemented as proposed.
expected_latency_quality_effects.quality_effect: Criteria explanations should stay intact, which is the main boundary the current proposal protects.

expected_latency_quality_effects.scope: Normal scope answers with a final-result caveat
expected_latency_quality_effects.latency_effect: No material change expected because the new branch should not trigger.
expected_latency_quality_effects.quality_effect: Normal answers should continue to include caveats without being collapsed into a refusal.

focused_rerun_plan.step: Run the existing guard fixture script to confirm the current sensitive-abstention and prompt-hint behavior still passes after the boundary change.
focused_rerun_plan.expected_check: Existing preempt and non-preempt boundary cases remain green, especially the current sensitive preempt and the non-adversarial boundary fixtures.

focused_rerun_plan.step: Add targeted smoke assertions for three query classes: unsupported final evaluation score, selected vendor / final award result, and evaluation-criteria explanation with the same vocabulary.
focused_rerun_plan.command: Use a temporary local Python smoke or the repo's existing fixture harness to call src.generator._preempt_sensitive_abstention_answer on synthetic queries before any commit-level change.
focused_rerun_plan.expected_check: Unsupported final-result queries short-circuit to the concise unavailable answer; criteria questions do not preempt; normal caveated scope questions still use the model path.

focused_rerun_plan.step: Re-run the local answer compare artifact for this run after the boundary change is implemented.
focused_rerun_plan.command: Inspect I:\0706\rfp-rag-project\eval\parallel_runs\20260707_125809_qv8-unsupported-award-result-refusal-boundary-repair\analysis\a11_answer_compare.json for before/after shifts in refusal length and false positives.
focused_rerun_plan.expected_check: Unsupported final-result cases get shorter and cleaner, while criteria cases and ordinary scope answers remain unchanged.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Broadening the existing sensitive marker list without a separate final-result discriminator
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: That would over-trigger on criteria/rubric questions and suppress legitimate explanation requests.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Moving the refusal logic into the general answer guard only
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: That still spends a model call on unsupported final-result questions and can preserve verbose hallucinated answers.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Making the guard fire on any query that mentions awards, vendors, or evaluation
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: Those terms appear in legitimate scope and criteria questions, so the false-positive rate would rise.
